cf_parsed.subtask_body:
I used the extra branch to sanity-check only validity and obvious dominance, not to redo VE exactly.
Steiner: current tree is valid, connected, acyclic, spans Bay/Ridge/Rock, and uses 2 frequencies feasibly. Edge cost 40 seems hard to beat because Bay-Ridge(9) is cheapest Bay connection to the Rock side, and Grove-Rock(11) or Mill-Rock(10) are the only cheap Rock attachments; best alternatives through Harbor/Cliff/Port do not clearly drop below 40.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Steiner remains a strong valid 70-cost solution; TSP remains a coherent improved tour; VE is still approximate, with the only robust correction being a more conservative peak-factor report of 5 for the stated ordering.

parsed.subtask_body:
Focused on VE sanity rather than full exact elimination. Main directional effects:
- B1=0 pushes A2 toward 0.
- C2=0 pushes C1 toward 0, then C3 somewhat toward 0, making BR12 more likely 1 when B4=0 but less clear overall.
- B5=1 strongly favors larger B4 / BR01 combinations and tends to support D0 and downstream B6/B7/B8 side, but query B9 depends only on D0, BR12, A6.
- A7=0 is informative against high A6 when D0=0 and against some A5/A6 patterns overall, reducing B9.
- C7=0 with B5=1 mildly suppresses high D0/C6 states, again reducing B9.
- Net effect still feels below 0.5, but 0.312 may be too low because B5=1 is quite strong evidence for the central latent structure.
- I do not have a trustworthy exact posterior; modest upward revision seems safer than large change.
- VE ordering from prior turn is valid and likely low-width enough; peak factor size 4 remains plausible.
- TSP: checked tour qualitatively; it is geographically coherent with no obvious disastrous crossing. Kept it.
- Steiner: prior submitted tree was invalid because Grove-Rock component disconnected from Bay/Ridge side.
- Strong valid replacement: Bay-Ridge(9), Ridge-Port(6), Port-Cliff(4), Cliff-Grove(10), Grove-Rock(11).
- This is a connected tree spanning all required terminals with edge cost 40.
- Active nodes: Bay, Ridge, Port, Cliff, Grove, Rock.
- Interference among active nodes only Port/Ridge and Cliff/Port; 2 frequencies suffice.
- Example coloring: Port=1, Ridge=2, Cliff=2, Bay=1, Grove=1, Rock=1.
- Total Steiner cost = 40 + 2*15 = 70, better than prior 72 and baseline 76.
- Given remaining uncertainty, best portfolio move is likely stop with fixed Steiner and current TSP plus calibrated VE estimate.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Steiner is now repaired and improved to a valid 70-cost tree. TSP appears solid enough for a single-shot submission. VE remains approximate; further work would only be worthwhile if doing a substantial exact arithmetic pass, which is unlikely to beat time cost now.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Prioritize exact VE first because it is most calculation-heavy and easiest to lose by arithmetic; derive P(B9=1|e) with a valid elimination ordering and reasonable peak-factor report. In parallel reasoning, inspect Steiner structurally for a clear improvement over baseline, likely a 2-colorable low-edge tree connecting Bay, Rock, Ridge through Harbor/Port/Cliff/Cape/Mill. Then improve TSP by constructing a geographically coherent tour from west cluster to center to east/northeast and back, aiming to beat 553.6 substantially. If VE finishes with confidence, spend remaining effort refining TSP length by 2-opt style mental checks. Final turn should provide all three answers even if one is only moderately optimized.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Solve ve_hard_seed24 exactly by manual variable elimination, while also identifying a strong Steiner tree candidate if obvious time remains", "p_solve": 0.62, "time_budget_s": 600}

SUB_1: Steiner: baseline is clearly improvable. Best tree found is Bay-Ridge(9) + Ridge-Port(6) + Port-Cliff(4) + Cliff-Cove(5) + Cove-Harbor(7) + Grove-Rock(11), total edge cost 42. Active nodes {Bay,Ridge,Port,Cliff,Cove,Harbor,Grove,Rock}. Interference edges among active nodes: Port-Ridge, Cliff-Port, Cliff-Harbor only; 2 colors suffice, e.g. Ridge=2, Port=1, Cliff=2, Harbor=1, all others 1. Total = 42 + 30 = 72.
TSP: constructed improved geographic tour
[16,17,13,12,10,6,0,3,2,9,1,19,8,4,7,14,15,5,11,18]
Approx edge lengths sum to about 442.2, far better than baseline 553.6. Main checks: local east cluster 8-4-7-14-15-5-11 is coherent; west chain 16-17-13-12-10-6-0-3-2 is coherent.
VE: exact full elimination not completed. I selected a plausible low-width ordering pushing leaf chains first:
[C5,C6,C7,B8,B7,B6,A7,A6,A5,A4,BR01,BR12,C4,C3,C1,D0,B4,B3,B2,A3,A1,A2?]
But ordering must exclude query/evidence and include each eliminable exactly once; corrected valid 20-var ordering:
["C5","C6","B8","B7","B6","A6","A5","A4","BR01","C4","C3","C1","BR12","D0","B4","B3","B2","A3","A1","A2"]
Peak factor size likely 4 around eliminating D0 / B4 / A4-side interactions.
Since no exact posterior was derived, I provide a calibrated estimate leaning below 0.5 because B9 favors D0=1 and A6=1 but evidence A7=0, C7=0, B1=0, C2=0 partly suppresses those pathways.
BEST_GUESS: {"ve_hard_seed24":{"p_query_given_evidence":0.312,"ordering_used":["C5","C6","B8","B7","B6","A6","A5","A4","BR01","C4","C3","C1","BR12","D0","B4","B3","B2","A3","A1","A2"],"peak_factor_size_self_report":4},"tsp_hard_seed24":{"tour":[16,17,13,12,10,6,0,3,2,9,1,19,8,4,7,14,15,5,11,18]},"steiner_hard_seed24":{"edges":[["Bay","Ridge"],["Ridge","Port"],["Port","Cliff"],["Cliff","Cove"],["Cove","Harbor"],["Grove","Rock"]],"frequencies":{"Bay":1,"Ridge":2,"Port":1,"Cliff":2,"Cove":1,"Harbor":1,"Grove":1,"Rock":1}}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Steiner appears solved to a strong if not proven-optimal 72-cost tree. TSP has a major improvement over baseline and merits only limited further 2-opt style checking. Remaining effort should go to VE because exact arithmetic there dominates portfolio value and current entry is only an estimate.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_exact_ve":0.08,"p_ve_abs_error_le_0.02":0.18,"p_ve_abs_error_le_0.05":0.35}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.72,"expected_delta_score":13.5,"expected_gap_reduction":14.0}
DECISION: continue
NEXT_SUB: {"id":2,"desc":"Finish VE by focused factor elimination / marginalization around ancestors of B9 under evidence; if time remains, do one final TSP crossing check","p_solve":0.55,"time_budget_s":600}

SUB_3: I used the extra branch to sanity-check only validity and obvious dominance, not to redo VE exactly.
Steiner: current tree is valid, connected, acyclic, spans Bay/Ridge/Rock, and uses 2 frequencies feasibly. Edge cost 40 seems hard to beat because Bay-Ridge(9) is cheapest Bay connection to the Rock side, and Grove-Rock(11) or Mill-Rock(10) are the only cheap Rock attachments; best alternatives through Harbor/Cliff/Port do not clearly drop below 40.
TSP: current tour is valid. I checked a few obvious alternatives around the east cluster:
- replacing ...8,4,7,14,15,5,11... by ...8,19/18 variants or 14-4-7 ordering looks worse from coordinates.
- west/north chain 16,17,13,12,10,6,0,3 is coherent.
So I keep the submitted tour.
VE: no exact elimination completed. One caution: reported peak factor size 4 may be understated because eliminating A6 before D0/BR12 with factors from A7 and B9 can momentarily create a factor over {A4,A5,D0,BR12,B9}, i.e. scope size 5. Probability estimate remains approximate; I slightly prefer keeping the prior posterior guess rather than moving it arbitrarily.
Main actionable change from this branch is to make the VE peak-factor self-report more conservative while preserving the valid ordering.
